trip.name: Yuval & Luciana in Japan
trip.description: The real itinerary. Arrive Tokyo Sep 19, then the 9 Booking.com hotel stays. Attractions/food/shopping entries are starter suggestions per zone — edit freely in the app or in this file.

zones.name: Hakone
zones.summary: Onsen town in the mountains — hot springs, the Hakone loop and (weather permitting) Mt. Fuji views over Lake Ashi.

zones.name: Fujikawaguchiko
zones.summary: Lake Kawaguchi at the foot of Mt. Fuji — the classic postcard views.

zones.name: Matsumoto
zones.summary: Castle town in the Japanese Alps — one-night stop between Fuji and Takayama.

zones.name: Takayama
zones.summary: Beautifully preserved old town in the Hida mountains — morning markets and Hida beef.

zones.name: Kanazawa
zones.summary: Edo-period districts, one of Japan's top three gardens, and a great food market.

zones.name: Kyoto
zones.summary: Temples, gardens, geisha districts and old streets — four full days.

zones.name: Osaka
zones.summary: Japan's kitchen — neon, street food and day-trip base (Nara, Kobe, Himeji).

zones.name: Tokyo
zones.summary: Arrival city and grand finale — Shinjuku, Shibuya, Asakusa and everything in between.

places.name: Hakone Yutowa
places.description: Sep 25 – 27 · Hakone · Booking.com, confirmed, free cancellation. Onsen resort with public baths. Option noted: upgrade to a superior room with private bath.

places.name: Mizno Hotel
places.description: Sep 27 – 28 · Fujikawaguchiko · Booking.com, confirmed, free cancellation. Option noted: upgrade to a superior room with balcony (lake/Fuji view).

places.name: Onyado Nono Matsumoto Natural Hot Spring
places.description: Sep 28 – 29 · Matsumoto · Booking.com, confirmed, free cancellation. Natural hot spring baths; parking info available on the reservation.

places.name: HOTEL WOOD TAKAYAMA
places.description: Sep 29 – Oct 1 · Takayama · Booking.com, confirmed, free cancellation. Parking info available on the reservation.

places.name: The HOTEL SANRAKU KANAZAWA
places.description: Oct 1 – 3 · Kanazawa · Booking.com, confirmed, free cancellation. Parking info and invoice available on the reservation.

places.name: Mitsui Garden Hotel Kyoto Shinmachi Bettei
places.name_ja: 三井ガーデンホテル京都新町別邸
places.description: Oct 3 – 7 · Kyoto · Booking.com, confirmed, free cancellation. Machiya-style hotel on Shinmachi street, central Kyoto.

places.name: Candeo Hotels Osaka Shinsaibashi
places.name_ja: カンデオホテルズ大阪心斎橋
places.description: Oct 7 – 11 · Osaka · Booking.com, confirmed. Rooftop sky spa; steps from Shinsaibashi shopping arcade and Dotonbori.

places.name: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
places.name_ja: ホテルグレイスリー新宿
places.description: Oct 11 – 16 · Tokyo (Shinjuku) · Booking.com, confirmed, free cancellation. Two reservations on file: Oct 11–16 and Oct 12–16. The Godzilla-head hotel in Kabukicho.

places.name: Owakudani
places.description: Volcanic valley on the Hakone ropeway — sulfur vents and the famous black eggs.

places.name: Hakone Open-Air Museum
places.description: Sculptures in the mountains, Picasso pavilion, and a foot bath.

places.name: Lake Kawaguchi
places.description: Lakeside walk and the Panoramic Ropeway; best Fuji reflections early morning.

places.name: Chureito Pagoda
places.description: THE Fuji photo spot — pagoda + Fuji in one frame. ~400 steps up; 20 min by train from Kawaguchiko.

places.name: Matsumoto Castle
places.description: One of Japan's most beautiful original castles — the black 'Crow Castle'. Evening light-up is lovely.

places.name: Sanmachi Suji old town
places.description: Preserved Edo-period streets — sake breweries, craft shops. Combine with the Miyagawa morning market by the river.

places.name: Kenroku-en Garden
places.description: One of Japan's three great gardens — go right at opening for near-empty paths. Kanazawa Castle is next door.

places.name: Higashi Chaya District
places.description: Geisha teahouse district — gold-leaf everything, including gold-leaf ice cream.

places.name: Omicho Market
places.description: Kanazawa's kitchen since 1721 — seafood breakfast (kaisendon) is the move.

places.name: Fushimi Inari Shrine
places.description: The thousand vermillion torii gates. Open 24h — sunrise or after 17:00 beats the crowds; full loop ~2h.

places.name: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
places.description: Bamboo forest walk — combine with Tenryu-ji temple and the monkey park. Go before 08:30.

places.name: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
places.description: The gold-leaf pavilion over its mirror pond. 30–45 min visit; pairs well with Ryoan-ji rock garden nearby.

places.name: Nishiki Market
places.description: 'Kyoto's kitchen' — knives, pickles, matcha sweets, street snacks. No eating while walking.

places.name: Matcha stop in Gion
places.description: Placeholder pick — matcha parfait/tea house in Gion; expect queues after 11:00.

places.name: Universal Studios Japan
places.name_ja: ユニバーサル・スタジオ・ジャパン
places.description: Oct 8 — our full USJ day (Halloween season + the park's 25th anniversary). Tickets go on sale ~2 months ahead — expect Aug 8–14, 2026 — and you must buy the day they open: there are NO ticket booths at the park anymore (online only, via Klook). Get 2× Studio Pass + 2× Universal Express Pass Premium for Oct 8. The day-by-day plan is in the schedule; the complete guide we made is attached below.

places.name: Dotonbori at night
places.description: Neon canal walk, Glico sign photo — 5 minutes from the hotel.

places.name: Osaka Castle
places.description: The castle park is the highlight — nice at golden hour; museum inside is skippable if short on time.

places.name: Okonomiyaki & takoyaki crawl
places.description: Placeholder pick — okonomiyaki dinner around Dotonbori/Namba, takoyaki stands for dessert.

places.name: Senso-ji Temple
places.description: Tokyo's oldest temple, in Asakusa. Before 09:00 beats the crowds on Nakamise street.

places.name: Shibuya Crossing & Sky
places.description: The scramble crossing at dusk; Shibuya Sky observation deck needs advance tickets.

places.name: teamLab Planets
places.name_ja: チームラボプラネッツ
places.description: Immersive digital art in Toyosu. Book ~2 weeks ahead; you walk barefoot through water — wear shorts.

places.name: Ramen night in Shinjuku
places.description: Placeholder pick — Omoide Yokocho alleys are next to the hotel; ticket machines, cash only.

places.name: Don Quijote Kabukicho
places.description: Chaotic everything-store around the corner from the hotel — souvenirs, snacks, tax-free with passport.

tips.body: Hakone Free Pass covers the whole loop (train, cable car, ropeway, pirate boat) and discounts many sights.
Use luggage forwarding (takkyubin) to send big bags ahead to a later hotel — travel the Fuji–Alps stretch light.
Fuji hides in clouds by late morning — plan viewpoints for early morning and check a Fuji visibility forecast.
Everything is walkable: station → Nakamachi street → castle in 20 minutes.
Hida beef is the local specialty — beef sushi at the market stalls, or a proper grill dinner.
The Kanazawa Loop Bus connects all the sights; day pass pays off with 3+ rides.
Buses get packed — prefer subway + walking; taxis are reasonable for 2 people on short hops.
Stand on the RIGHT on escalators in Osaka (opposite of Tokyo).
Get a Suica/Pasmo in Apple/Google Wallet for all trains, metro and convenience stores.
Trash cans are rare — carry a small bag for garbage.
Onsen etiquette: wash sitting down before entering the bath; towels stay out of the water.
Ask for a high floor on the Godzilla side; the head 'roars' on schedule from the 8F terrace.
Open 24h — sunrise or after 17:00 for photos without crowds.

Buy tickets the day they open (~Aug 8–14 for Oct 8). No booths at the park since May 2025 — online only, via Klook (foreign-card friendly). Premium Express passes sell out first, especially in Halloween season.

Express Pass Premium: if offered as P25 (incl. Space Fantasy) vs P29, prefer P25. With Premium, Nintendo World entry is guaranteed at any time. Passes are non-refundable, non-changeable and personal — no sharing.

Download the official USJ app for wait times, show times and the map. You can exit any queue before boarding. Check the crowd forecast at usjreal.asumirai.info (A=empty, F=packed). Save tickets as PDF + QR screenshots — signal is bad near the park.

itinerary.title: Pick up the rental car at 11:00
Today you're going to Universal Studios!
🎂 Luciana's Birthday
itinerary.note: Two treats in one day (spa + Maguro Mart) both benefit from advance booking — lock the spa time first, then pick a Maguro Mart slot that leaves comfortable travel time between them.

itinerary.title: ★ The Head Spa — birthday treat
itinerary.note: Branches in Shibuya (2 min from Shibuya Stn Exit C2) and Ebisu/Daikanyama; the Ebisu one is the exclusive, in-demand studio (only ~5 slots/day, book via Instagram DM). Book well ahead — these fill up 1–2 months out, and a birthday slot shouldn't be left to chance.

itinerary.title: Relaxed lunch & neighbourhood wander
itinerary.note: Keep the day soft and celebratory — a relaxed lunch, then wander a pretty neighbourhood (Daikanyama / Nakameguro cafés are lovely if you spa in that area).

itinerary.title: ★ Maguro Mart — birthday dinner
itinerary.note: Evening. Tuna/seafood izakaya — a fun birthday dinner. Popular, so reserve or arrive early.

itinerary.title: 🎢 Universal Studios — at the gates
itinerary.note: Take the JR Yumesaki line to Universal City (~15 min from Osaka Stn). Gates usually open before the official time. Sleep in Osaka the night before, not Kyoto.

itinerary.title: Super Nintendo World (morning)
itinerary.note: Do it as one block: Mario Kart, Mine Cart Madness, Yoshi, Power Up Band games, Kinopio's Cafe. Leaving the area = no re-entry, so finish everything first.

itinerary.title: Split, then Jurassic Park & JAWS
itinerary.note: Yuval: Flying Dinosaur, Hollywood Dream + Backdrop. Luciana: break / shopping. Together: Jurassic Park (sit mid-boat — you get wet) and JAWS (day version).

itinerary.title: Wizarding World of Harry Potter
itinerary.note: Castle walk, wand ceremony at Ollivanders, butterbeer, Hippogriff / Forbidden Journey by feel. Then Minions + the Waterworld show.

itinerary.title: Halloween evening (together)
itinerary.note: Street zombies, Zombie de Dance, Resident Evil: Requiem – The Dive (walkthrough), Sadako's Curse, and close with JAWS: Red Alert. Quiet zones if needed: Nintendo World and the family area.

itinerary.title: Dinner at Universal CityWalk
itinerary.note: After the park closes — grab dinner at CityWalk on the way back to the train.
